message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Best Stats: Optimal Builds & Guide 2026",
  description: "Master the meta with our Bizarre Lineage best stats guide. Learn optimal distributions for White Snake, King Crimson, and Anubis to dominate in 2026.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/ffffff?text=Bizarre+Lineage+Best+Stats",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Lineage Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Bizarre lineage best stats**: Focus on **Destructive Power** for physical stands and **Weapon** for sword-based builds.
- **Vitality threshold**: Always aim for at least **25-30 Health** to survive high-level NPC combos and world events.
- **Energy management**: Invest in **Destructive Energy** only if your Stand relies heavily on special AoE or ranged abilities.
- **Stat resets**: Use **Stat Point Essence** from Rahaj’s Shop (10k Cash) if you need to reallocate points for a new Stand.
- **Meta builds**: King Crimson requires high **Destructive Power (120)**, while C-Moon prioritizes **Destructive Energy (100)**.
</Callout>

Navigating the dangerous streets of Morioh Cho in Bizarre Lineage requires more than just a powerful Stand; it demands a deep understanding of how to allocate your limited Stat Points. Whether you are aiming to dominate in PvP or survive high-level raids, finding the **bizarre lineage best stats** for your specific build is essential for progression. This guide provides a comprehensive breakdown of every attribute, from Destructive Power to Weapon mastery, ensuring you never waste a Stat Point Essence again. We will explore optimal distributions for top-tier Stands like White Snake and King Crimson, while also detailing how accessories and fighting styles influence your overall power. By following our expert recommendations for **bizarre lineage best stats**, you can maximize your damage output and survivability in 2026. Let's dive into the mechanics that define the current meta and help you surpass your limits.

<Section icon={Map} title="Core Statistics Explained">

Understanding what each attribute actually does is the first step to creating a viable build. Many players confuse Strength with Destructive Power, leading to wasted points that do not benefit their Stand's performance.

**Video Highlights:**
- **Strength vs DP**: Strength only affects your character's M1s, while Destructive Power affects your Stand's physical attacks.
- **Power Usage**: Power acts as your stamina bar and boosts Sub-Ability damage (Hamon/Vampire).
- **Weapon Scaling**: Essential for Anubis users and those utilizing stop signs or axes.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="31xpLtKa-0w" title="Stat Guide In BIZARRE LINEAGE!" />

| Stat | Primary Function | Ideal For |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Strength** | Increases Physical based attacks (Player) | Standless, Boxing, or Karate builds |
| **Health** | Increases Maximum HP | Everyone (Vital for survival) |
| **Power** | Increases Sub-Ability DMG and Max Power | Hamon, Vampire, and Cyborg users |
| **Weapon** | Increases Slash and Weapon Damage | Anubis, Kendo, and Sword users |
| **Destructive Power** | Increases Stand’s Physical attacks | Star Platinum, The World, King Crimson |
| **Destructive Energy** | Increases Stand’s Special Ability DMG | White Snake, C-Moon, Weather Report |

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Tip">
If you are using a Stand-focused build, ignore the Strength stat entirely. Your Stand's M1 damage scales exclusively off **Destructive Power**, not your character's physical Strength.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Best Stats for Every Stand">

The meta in 2026 favors specialized builds over balanced ones. Depending on your Stand's kit, you should focus your points into one or two primary damage stats while maintaining a baseline for health.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="White Snake">
    - **Health**: 25-30
    - **Destructive Power**: 100
    - **Destructive Energy**: 25
    - *Focus on move damage and mode bar.*
  </Card>
  <Card title="King Crimson">
    - **Health**: 25
    - **Destructive Power**: 120
    - **Destructive Energy**: 5
    - *Maximum physical burst damage.*
  </Card>
  <Card title="Anubis">
    - **Health**: 30
    - **Destructive Power**: 90
    - **Weapon**: 40-80
    - *Hybrid scaling for sword combos.*
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

For more specialized Stands, the distribution changes significantly to account for energy costs or unique mechanics.

| Stand Name | HP | Destructive Power | Destructive Energy | Weapon |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Made in Heaven** | 30 | 90 | 35 | 0 |
| **C-Moon** | 50 | 0 | 100 | 0 |
| **The World** | 25 | 105 | 10 | 0 |
| **TW High Voltage** | 25 | 55 | 70 | 0 |

<Callout type="warning" title="Meta Warning">
C-Moon is a unique case where **Destructive Energy** is significantly more valuable than physical power. Putting points into DP for C-Moon is generally considered a waste of resources.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Sub-Abilities and Fighting Styles">

While your Stand provides the bulk of your power, Fighting Styles and Sub-Abilities provide essential utility and secondary damage. These systems often require **Power** to function effectively.

### Fighting Styles Overview
Fighting styles are purchased from various coaches located around Morioh Cho. They focus on melee utility and can bridge the gap when your Stand is on cooldown.

| Style | Key Moves | Best Paired With |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Boxing** | White Fang, Dempsey Roll | High HP/Strength builds |
| **Karate** | Seiken Barrage, Tatsumaki | Defensive/Counter playstyles |
| **Kendo** | Whirlwind, Silent Edge | Anubis or Weapon-based builds |

### Sub-Abilities (The Ripple & Beyond)
Sub-Abilities offer passive buffs and active skills that can turn the tide of a battle.

- **Hamon**: Offers passive HP regeneration. Essential for long PvE sessions.
- **Cyborg**: Provides a +15% Defense buff, making you much tankier in PvP.
- **Vampire**: High HP regen but carries a weakness to sunlight unless specialized items are used.

<Callout type="success" title="Build Synergy">
Pairing **Cyborg** with a high **Health** build (40+) makes you an immovable object in raids, allowing you to tank boss hits that would normally one-shot other players.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Accessory Stat Boosts & Multipliers">

Accessories are the hidden layer of the **bizarre lineage best stats** meta. Each item you equip can roll specific stat boosts that stack with your base attributes. The multiplier for these boosts is determined by the item's rarity.

| Rarity | Multiplier |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Common** | 1.0x |
| **Uncommon** | 1.5x |
| **Rare** | 2.0x |
| **Legendary** | 2.5x |
| **Mythical** | 3.0x |

### Accessory Slot Focus
Different slots provide different types of bonuses. Use this to fill gaps in your build:
- **Head/Back/Body**: Primarily focus on **HP** and **Defense**.
- **Face/Legs**: Great for **Power** and **Power Regen**.
- **Neck/Arms**: The best source for **Penetration** and **HP Regen**.

<Callout type="info" title="Optimization">
Always aim for Legendary or Mythical accessories for your Body and Head slots. The 2.5x to 3.0x multiplier on HP can grant you hundreds of extra health points without spending a single Stat Point.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Progression and Awakening">

To reach the endgame, you must progress through the Prestige system and eventually awaken your Stand. This process requires reaching the level cap and completing difficult challenges.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Reach Level 50">
    Grind NPCs, complete quests, and participate in World Events to hit the level cap. This is required for both Prestige and Awakening.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Prestige at TC Hospital">
    Visit the Arch Mage NPC at Bus Stop 10. You need 10,000 Cash. Each Prestige level grants 5 Prestige Points and a Stand Storage slot.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Max Stand Conjuration">
    Use your Stand constantly to reach Conjuration Level 100. This unlocks the ability to begin the Awakening quests.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Complete Awakening Quests">
    Talk to the NPC at the Gym (Bus Stop 2). You must defeat Jotaro, Avdol, and DIO in raids, then participate in 50 World Events.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Callout type="warning" title="Reset Warning">
When you Prestige, your level resets to 1. Ensure you have enough Stat Point Essences or Cash to quickly rebuild your character after the reset.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Endgame Checklist">

Use this checklist to ensure your character is fully optimized for the 2026 meta.

<Checklist
  id="endgame-optimization"
  title="Bizarre Lineage Mastery Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Reach Prestige 3 for maximum Stand Storage",
    "Obtain a Mythical accessory for the Body slot",
    "Unlock Stand Awakening (Ultimate) via the Gym NPC",
    "Equip a Sub-Ability that complements your Stand (e.g., Hamon for White Snake)",
    "Allocate Stat Points based on the 2026 meta distributions"
  ]}
/>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What are the bizarre lineage best stats for a beginner?",
    answer: "Beginners should focus on a balance of Health (20-30) and Destructive Power (max). This allows you to survive NPC encounters while dealing enough damage to clear quests quickly."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I reset my stats if I make a mistake?",
    answer: "You can purchase Stat Point Essence from Rahaj’s Shop for 10,000 Cash. It is also available in the Prestige Shop and the Robux Shop."
  },
  {
    question: "Does Strength affect my Stand's barrage damage?",
    answer: "No. Strength only affects your character's basic M1 attacks. Stand barrages and heavy punches scale exclusively with Destructive Power."
  },
  {
    question: "How many times can I Prestige in Bizarre Lineage?",
    answer: "You can Prestige up to three times. Each time grants you more storage and Prestige Points to spend on exclusive essences."
  }
]} />

</Section>
